_note: schema-constructed (not real-captured). Shape and field names match the live PatentsView PatentSearch API (https://search.patentsview.org/api/v1/patent/, verified 2026-06-15): top-level { error, count, total_hits, patents[] }; patent fields patent_id/patent_title/patent_abstract/patent_date; nested groups assignees[].assignee_organization, inventors[].inventor_name_first/inventor_name_last, cpc_current[].cpc_subclass_id/cpc_group_id. The live API requires a free X-Api-Key, which was unavailable in this environment, and outbound network from the shell is blocked, so a representative response for the query 'self watering planter float valve' was constructed to the confirmed schema rather than recorded live.

patents.patent_title: Self-watering planter with float-actuated shutoff valve
patents.patent_abstract: A self-watering planter includes a water reservoir, a float disposed within the reservoir, and a shutoff valve mechanically coupled to the float. As water is added, the float rises and closes the valve when the water reaches a predetermined level, preventing overflow and maintaining a substantially constant reservoir level for sub-irrigation of soil above the reservoir.

patents.patent_title: Float valve assembly for sub-irrigated plant container
patents.patent_abstract: A float valve assembly for a plant container maintains a target water level in a lower reservoir. A buoyant float actuates a poppet valve seated against an inlet; when the reservoir level falls, the float drops and the valve opens to admit water from a supply line, and when the target level is reached the float closes the valve.

patents.patent_title: Wicking self-watering plant container with reservoir level indicator
patents.patent_abstract: A self-watering plant container draws water from a reservoir to a growing medium via a capillary wick. A transparent sight tube communicating with the reservoir indicates the water level so that the reservoir can be refilled before depletion, reducing the need for a mechanical float valve.
